title: Rolling Cubby Bench
summary: A low 1x12 bench with three open cubbies and casters — a mudroom seat, toy bin, or window bench that rolls where you need it.
description:
A simple, useful piece: a shallow bench-height box divided into three cubbies, wearing a 1x2 trim face and riding on casters. It works as a seat with baskets below, a rolling toy store, or a window bench you can move to sweep behind. The whole thing is 1x12 lumber and finish nails — a genuine one-evening build.

The trim does more than dress the front. When you fasten the top trim into the dividers, sides, and top all at once, you're reinforcing those butt joints, which is what lets a 1x12 box take someone sitting on it. Keep the outside edges flush and center the vertical trim pieces on the dividers so the face reads as intentional cubbies, not random slats.

The wheel supports get 'measured to fit' — cut them to bridge under the box where the casters mount so the wheels have solid backing instead of pulling out of a single thickness of 1x12. Fill the nail holes, sand, and finish; a mudroom bench earns its scuffs, so a durable topcoat pays off.

tools.note: The box and trim go together with 2" nails and glue.

cutList.note: Measure to fit perfectly between the horizontal trim.
Measure to fit; back the casters.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one top at 48", one bottom at 46-1/2", two sides at 15-1/2" and two dividers at 14" from the 1x12. Cut four horizontal trim at 48", eight vertical trim at 13-1/4" and two wheel supports at 11-1/2" from the 1x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x12 and four 8 ft 1x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Assemble the top, bottom, sides, and two dividers into a three-cubby box with 2" nails and glue. Keep it square so the trim lands evenly.

steps.title: Add the top and bottom trim
steps.body: Fasten the horizontal trim pieces with top and outside edges flush, using glue and 2" nails. Nail the top trim into the dividers, sides, and top together — this ties the joints and lets the bench carry weight.

steps.title: Add the vertical trim
steps.body: Fasten the vertical trim with outside edges flush, centering the middle pieces on the dividers, and also nail through the ends into the horizontal trim above and below. This is what makes the three cubbies read cleanly.

steps.title: Wheel supports, casters, and finish
steps.body: Fasten the wheel supports under the box where the casters mount so the wheels have solid backing, then screw on the four casters. Fill nail holes, sand, and finish with a durable topcoat — this bench gets used hard.

images.alt: Low rolling bench with three open cubbies and casters
